this is a Geometry3K circle-geometry item, where point placement and labeled arc/angle relations are typically conveyed visually
multi_step: solving likely requires combining the diameter fact with circle arc/angle relationships rather than reading a single value directly
the target is m(arc QRT), suggesting identification of a major arc or composed arc from labeled points, which is at least a two-step inference
the problem is still a short single-target geometry item, so multi-step demand appears moderate rather than very high
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('255'), which is easy to compare against a gold answer
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and image asset exists, so the sample is structurally complete
geometry reasoning for arc measures is usually checkable by explicit angle/arc rules, supporting process-level verification
